data.title: Why Is Exxon Mobil Stock Falling Today?
data.description:
Exxon Mobil Corporation (NYSE:XOM) shares traded lower on Wednesday as geopolitical tensions disrupted operations.

The oil and gas behemoth said in an exchange filing that it expects Middle East disruptions to reduce first-quarter upstream earnings by $300 million to $500 million.

The company also sees a $100 million to $300 million hit to its energy products segment.

Broader Market Backdrop

Oil and gas stocks moved lower after President Donald Trump signaled progress toward a U.S.-Iran agreement, easing fears of prolonged supply disruptions.

The de-escalation outlook pressured oil prices as expectations of improved Middle East supply and reduced geopolitical risk weighed on the sector.

Exxon Mobil outlined multiple factors expected to influence first-quarter 2026 results, including supply interruptions and pricing dynamics.

ExxonMobil said instability in the Middle East affected production volumes and logistics during the quarter.

These disruptions, combined with market factors and seasonal trends, impacted performance.

Middle East Disruptions Pressure Output

ExxonMobil said assets in Qatar and the U.A.E faced operational interruptions beginning in March. These regions accounted for about one-fifth of global production in 2025.

The company expects these disruptions to reduce global oil-equivalent output by roughly 6% compared with the prior quarter.

Damage to two LNG facilities in Qatar contributed to the decline. Management indicated repairs may take considerable time but has not confirmed a recovery timeline.

The affected facilities represented a small share of prior production but remain strategically important.

Refining And Supply Constraints

ExxonMobil also flagged reduced crude availability affecting downstream operations. The company expects global refining and product throughput to decline modestly.

Middle East assets represent a limited share of refining capacity, yet disruptions impacted Asia Pacific operations. The company expects energy product throughput to fall about 2% sequentially.

Market And Timing Factors Impact Earnings

ExxonMobil highlighted commodity price changes as a major driver of first-quarter performance. Higher oil and gas prices supported earnings, though not uniformly across segments.

However, accounting timing effects linked to hedging activities weighed heavily on reported earnings. These effects stem from valuation differences between derivatives and physical shipments.

The company estimated negative timing impacts in the billions compared to the previous quarter. These effects typically reverse over time but create short-term earnings volatility.

Outlook And Strategic Actions

ExxonMobil said it continues to leverage its integrated global system to navigate disruptions. The company is increasing Permian Basin output to offset supply challenges.

It is also optimizing logistics and maximizing refinery utilization where feasible. The Golden Pass LNG project reached initial production, marking a key milestone.

XOM Price Action: Exxon Mobil shares were down 6.15% at $153.83 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Pony AI Debuts Commercial Robotaxi Rides In Zagreb
data.description:
Details

This significant milestone is part of Pony AI’s strategy to expand its autonomous vehicle operations, as it continues to grow its fleet and market presence.

The company aims to grow its fleet to 3,000 vehicles by the end of the year, following recent milestones in China that validate its business model.

Dr. James Peng, Founder and CEO of Pony AI said, “As we advance our dual-engine growth strategy, Zagreb represents both our first commercial robotaxi service in Europe and a meaningful validation of our Gen-7 robotaxi capabilities in a new market.”

PONY at $9.48; RSI 29.47 Oversold

The broader market saw gains on Tuesday, with the Technology sector rising 4.21%. Pony AI’s rise comes as the broader sector moved higher, indicating a favorable environment for tech stocks.

At $9.48, the stock is trading 10.1% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) of $10.57, suggesting a bearish short-term trend. It is also 24.6% below its 50-day SMA of $12.60, indicating continued weakness in the intermediate trend.

The relative strength index (RSI) is at 29.47, which is in oversold territory, suggesting that the stock may be undervalued and could see a rebound if buying interest increases.

  • Key Resistance: $10.50 — A level where selling pressure has historically emerged.
  • Key Support: $8.00 — A critical level that could indicate a reversal if tested.

Recent Earnings

Last month, Pony AI reported fourth-quarter revenue of $29.1 million, down 18% year-over-year due to timing in licensing and applications revenue. Street analysts expected a quarterly revenue of $23.94 million.

Adjusted EPS loss of 12 cents per share, down from 23 cents loss in the prior year’s quarter. The Street had projected an EPS of 22 cents.

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $21.17. Recent analyst moves include:

  • HSBC: Initiated with Buy (Target $16.60) (March 31)
  • Barclays: Equal-Weight (Lowers Target to $10.00) (March 30)
  • Barclays: Initiated with Equal-Weight (Target $15.00) (Dec. 17, 2025)

Benzinga Edge: PONY Momentum Score 83/100

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Pony AI, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Bullish (Score: 83/100) — Stock is outperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Pony AI’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a momentum-driven story, suggesting strong performance relative to market peers. This positive momentum could attract further investor interest as the company expands its operations.

PONY ETF Weights: CABZ 4.36%, HAIL 2.34%

  • SPDR S&P Kensho Smart Mobility ETF (NYSE:HAIL): 2.34% Weight
  • Roundhill Robotaxi, Autonomous Vehicles & Technology ETF (NASDAQ:CABZ): 4.36% Weight

Significance: Because PONY carries significant weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

data.title: AtaiBeckley Depression Drug Delivers Rapid Day-2 Antidepressant Response
data.description:
Depression Drug Shows Sustained Antidepressant Response

The company reported a 66.7% antidepressant response rate at Day 2, which has contributed to the stock's upward momentum as broader markets experienced gains on Tuesday.

AtaiBeckley announced that a single intranasal dose of BPL-003 achieved significant reductions in MADRS scores among participants with treatment-resistant depression, with 83% of patients maintaining a response at Week 12.

BPL-003 was generally well tolerated with no serious adverse events reported, and participants achieved a mean discharge time of approximately 100 minutes post-dose.

The company is on track to initiate Phase 3 studies in the second quarter of 2026, following recent FDA alignment.

Pipeline Progress Includes EMP-01 In Social Anxiety Disorder

In February, AtaiBeckley shared topline results from an exploratory Phase 2a trial of EMP-01, an oral R-MDMA treatment for Social Anxiety Disorder.

In the trial, 49% of patients receiving EMP-01 were rated as "very much improved" or "much improved," compared to only 15% in the placebo group, indicating a clinically meaningful improvement.

AtaiBeckley Technical Setup Signals Near-Term Momentum With Mixed Trend

At $4.09, the stock is trading 12.8% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 6% above its 100-day SMA, suggesting strong short-term momentum.

However, the 50-day SMA is still below the current price, indicating a mixed trend in the intermediate term.

The relative strength index (RSI) is at 53.63, which is neutral and suggests that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold. This positioning indicates a balanced buyer-seller dynamic, allowing for potential upward movement if positive sentiment continues.

  • Key Resistance: $4.50 — A level where selling pressure may increase.
  • Key Support: $3.50 — A level where buying interest could emerge.

The stock has shown a remarkable 12-month performance of 213.22%, reflecting strong long-term growth potential.

Currently, it is trading near the midpoint of its 52-week range, which suggests that while there is room for growth, the stock is not at an extreme high or low.

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $14.20. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Deutsche Bank: Initiated with Buy (Target $12.00) (March 27)
  • HC Wainwright & Co.: Buy (Maintains Target to $25.00) (March 17)
  • Needham: Buy (Maintains Target to $14.00) (March 9)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for AtaiBeckley, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Bullish (Score: 91.36) — Stock is outperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: AtaiBeckley's Benzinga Edge signal reveals a strong momentum-driven story, reflecting its recent positive developments and potential for growth in a challenging sector.

ATAI Price Action: AtaiBeckley shares were up 0.13% at $3.79 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Why AT&T Shares Are Falling Wednesday
data.description:
West Texas Intermediate crude dropped 17% to about $93 a barrel early Wednesday, triggering a broad relief rally and sending traders back into risk-sensitive groups such as airlines, cruises and other conflict-hit names.

  • AT&T stock is trending lower. Why are T shares declining?

AT&T's Stable Cash-Flow Profile Can Limit Risk-On Appeal

Wednesday’s backdrop can pressure AT&T even without company-specific news. AT&T is a mature telecom operator whose business is built around relatively stable wireless-service revenue, fiber broadband growth and recurring subscriber cash flows.

Because of that profile, the stock is often treated less like a high-growth trade and more like a defensive, income-oriented name investors hold for steadier performance and dividend support.

Risk-On Rotation Can Weigh On Defensive Telecom Stocks

When geopolitical tension eases and oil collapses, the market often moves the other way: money comes out of defensive stocks and rotates into names with more direct upside to lower fuel costs, improving consumer sentiment and a broader risk-on tape. Wednesday's setup fits that pattern.

In other words, AT&T appears to be falling not because lower oil hurts its operations directly, but because the ceasefire-driven rally is making its slower, defensive earnings profile look less urgent versus sectors with more immediate rebound leverage.

Analyst Price Targets Point To More AT&T Upside

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy rating with an average price target of $30.55. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Scotiabank: Sector Perform (Raises Target to $31.50) (April 1)
  • Keybanc: Overweight (Raises Target to $36.00) (March 25)
  • Citigroup: Buy (Raises Target to $31.50) (March 23)

T Shares Slide Wednesday

T Price Action: AT&T shares were down 3.00% at $27.20 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.
